think_blocks:
The user wants me to segment the video into 7-second intervals.

1.  **Analyze the video length:** The video is 118.6 seconds long.
2.  **Calculate segments:**
    *   118.6 / 7 = 16.94
    *   This means there will be 16 full segments of 7 seconds, and one final segment for the remainder.
    *   16 * 7 = 112 seconds.
    *   Remaining time: 118.6 - 112 = 6.6 seconds.

3.  **Define segment boundaries:**
    *   Segment 1: 00:00 - 00:07
    *   Segment 2: 00:07 - 00:14
    *   Segment 3: 00:14 - 00:21
    *   Segment 4: 00:21 - 00:28
    *   Segment 5: 00:28 - 00:35
    *   Segment 6: 00:35 - 00:42
    *   Segment 7: 00:42 - 00:49
    *   Segment 8: 00:49 - 00:56
    *   Segment 9: 00:56 - 01:03
    *   Segment 10: 01:03 - 01:10
    *   Segment 11: 01:10 - 01:17
    *   Segment 12: 01:17 - 01:24
    *   Segment 13: 01:24 - 01:31
    *   Segment 14: 01:31 - 01:38
    *   Segment 15: 01:38 - 01:45
    *   Segment 16: 01:45 - 01:52
    *   Segment 17: 01:52 - 01:58.6

4.  **Extract transcript and summary for each segment:**

    *   **Segment 1 (00:00 - 00:07):**
        *   Transcript: "I came from a place very far away. I live in South Korea."
        *   Summary: Bong Joon Ho begins his acceptance speech, mentioning his origins in South Korea.

    *   **Segment 2 (00:07 - 00:14):**
        *   Transcript: "So in campus, we held several illegal film festivals where we would have unofficial screenings at local cinematechs."
        *   Summary: He recounts holding illegal film festivals during his college days.

    *   **Segment 3 (00:14 - 00:21):**
        *   Transcript: "이제 저희가 미국 인디펜던트 영화들을 많이 영화제에서 틀고 저희가 직접 서브 타이틀도 자막도 넣고 그랬단 말이죠." (Now we screened a lot of American independent films at film festivals and we put in subtitles and captions ourselves.)
        *   Summary: He explains in Korean that they screened American indie films and created their own subtitles.

    *   **Segment 4 (00:21 - 00:28):**
        *   Transcript: "At the time, we would screen a lot of American indie films and create subtitles ourselves."
        *   Summary: The translator conveys his story about screening indie films and making subtitles.

    *   **Segment 5 (00:28 - 00:35):**
        *   Transcript: "The movie I did subtitle was Thank you."
        *   Summary: He reveals the specific movie he subtitled, thanking the audience.

    *   **Segment 6 (00:35 - 00:42):**
        *   Transcript: "Thank you. Let's go."
        *   Summary: He thanks the audience again and prepares to continue his story.

    *   **Segment 7 (00:42 - 00:49):**
        *   Transcript: "영어 를 그때도 영어 실력이 부족한 상태에서 그거 를 서비타이틀 열심히 했는데" (At that time, even though my English skills were lacking, I worked hard on subtitling that.)
        *   Summary: He speaks in Korean about struggling with English while subtitling.

    *   **Segment 8 (00:49 - 00:56):**
        *   Transcript: "와 그렇게 다양한 욕들이 있는지 처음 알았어요 스파이클이 덕분에 그걸 많이 배웠어요" (Wow, I didn't know there were such various curse words. Thanks to Spike Lee, I learned a lot.)
        *   Summary: He humorously mentions learning many curse words from Spike Lee's film.

    *   **Segment 9 (00:56 - 01:03):**
        *   Transcript: "So at the time, my English wasn't that good, so subtitling it was quite an experience. I had no idea there was such various curse words in the English language. Thanks to Spike Lee, I learned so many things."
        *   Summary: The translator explains his English struggles and the lesson learned from Spike Lee.

    *   **Segment 10 (01:03 - 01:10):**
        *   Transcript: (No speech, mostly visual of translator speaking and Bong listening)
        *   Summary: The translator finishes her sentence while Bong Joon Ho listens attentively.

    *   **Segment 11 (01:10 - 01:17):**
        *   Transcript: (No speech, Bong Joon Ho starts speaking)
        *   Summary: Bong Joon Ho prepares to speak again after the translation.

    *   **Segment 12 (01:17 - 01:24):**
        *   Transcript: "너무 음악 흥분이 되고 영광스러워서 급하게 사진을 찍었죠. 같이 사진 찍고 그랬는데 내가 Do the Right Sing 자막을 했었다 그 얘기는 못했어요." (I was so excited and honored that I took a picture in a hurry. We took a picture together, but I couldn't tell him that I subtitled Do the Right Thing.)
        *   Summary: He explains in Korean that he was too excited to tell Spike Lee about the subtitling.

    *   **Segment 13 (01:24 - 01:31):**
        *   Transcript: "지금 다시 만나면 그 얘기를 하고 싶어요. 누구 스파이클이 아는 분 있으면 그 얘기 좀 전해주세요." (If I meet him again, I want to tell him that story. If anyone knows Spike Lee, please tell him that story.)
        *   Summary: He expresses his desire to tell Spike Lee the story if they meet again.

    *   **Segment 14 (01:31 - 01:38):**
        *   Transcript: "So I was so excited and honored, I made sure to take a picture right away, but things were so hectic I didn't get the chance to tell him I subtitled Do the Right Thing."
        *   Summary: The translator conveys his excitement and missed opportunity to speak with Spike Lee.

    *   **Segment 15 (01:38 - 01:45):**
        *   Transcript: "If I meet him again, I would love to tell him. If any of you guys are acquainted with him, please let him know."
        *   Summary: She asks the audience to pass the message to Spike Lee.

    *   **Segment 16 (01:45 - 01:52):**
        *   Transcript: "Thank you, thank you everyone."
        *   Summary: Bong Joon Ho thanks the audience and concludes his speech.

    *   **Segment 17 (01:52 - 01:58.6):**
        *   Transcript: (No speech, applause and title card)
        *   Summary: The audience applauds as the title card for the 11th Annual AAFCA Awards appears.

5.  **Final Review:** Check timestamps and content alignment. The segmentation looks correct based on the 7-second rule.
